purge.title: Permanent data removal

Global clearing remains available for emergencies.

fees.lead: Set up fee items and class plans, assign them to students, and track collections in one place.
fees.exportFailed: Could not export fee structures CSV.
fees.exportTimeout: Export is taking longer than expected. Please retry shortly.
fees.bannerParentCheckoutOff: Fee collection is set to pay at school (offline). Record counter payments here; switch finance mode when you are ready for parent-portal online checkout.
fees.v2StructuresDesc: Build the fee package for each class: add lines from your fee items, set amounts, and save. Then assign the plan to students.
fees.v2AssignmentsDesc: Choose the class and student, pick the fee plan they should follow, and set the start date. The plan list only shows versions created for that class. If no plan appears, you can enter the plan reference from your records.
fees.v2ManualPlanHint: Use this only when no plan appears in the list—for example if the plan was created elsewhere.
fees.v2VersionFromPlanHint: Taken from the plan you selected.
fees.v2StudentIdsCsvHelp: If you fill this in, the class and section above are ignored. Use this for a fixed list from a spreadsheet.
fees.v2RunIdempotencyHelp: Use any unique label (for example April-rules-2026). Running again with the same label will not duplicate assignments.
fees.v2PreviewSummary: {{change}} of {{total}} students would get a different plan.
fees.v2CurrentAssignmentsDesc: Review saved assignments. Filter by student or refresh after you make changes.
fees.v3StatementLead: Pick a student to see open charges, recent account entries, and running balance.
fees.v3PayRegStudentHint: Leave as “whole school” for everyone, or choose a class and then a student to narrow the list. Dates always apply.
fees.v2RuleApplySectionLead: Set effective dates and a unique run reference, then execute. Use a new reference whenever you intend a fresh run; repeating the same reference is safe and will not duplicate work.
fees.v2DemandsDesc: Amounts posted after a billing run. Filter by student to see balance and status.
fees.v2DiscountsDesc: Flat amount or percentage off. Apply to every fee item, or only selected items (use the ID list when you need a subset).
fees.v2StructureLinesInvalid: Add at least one line with a component and a positive amount.
fees.v2RuleLogicDesc: Fine-tune when this rule applies and what it should do. Most schools set this up once with support.
fees.v3ReportsDesc: Payments received, students who still owe, and class totals for the period you choose. Leave dates empty to use the full academic year.
fees.v3PaymentsDesc: Recorded fee payments with optional filters (up to 500 rows).
fees.v3AuditDesc: History of billing runs, payments, and refunds (latest 200).
fees.v4LateDesc: Set how many grace days you allow and whether late fees are a fixed amount or a percentage. Then apply them for the date you choose.
fees.v4RunsDesc: Choose a policy and the date to apply through. Using the same run reference again returns the same result so families are not charged twice.
fees.v3RefundDesc: Adds the amount back to what the family owes. Use a reference key so the same refund is not posted twice.
fees.v3RefundSubmitApproval: Submit for approval only (no balance change until approved)
fees.v3ReconDesc: Students where amount due and account balance do not match—for staff review (up to 100). Nothing is changed automatically.
fees.v3RazorpayOrderDesc: Starts an online card/UPI payment. When the family pays, the receipt and balance update automatically.
fees.v2RuleAssignmentDesc: Preview your automation rules: see who would switch to another fee plan, then run the update for the dates you choose. Pick a class (and section if needed), or paste a list of student numbers.
fees.v2ComponentsDesc: List each type of fee your school charges (for example tuition, transport, activities). You reuse these when building class plans.
fees.v2RulesDesc: Control how plans are assigned, how discounts apply, and how late fees work.
fees.v2DemandRunsDesc: Post charges for every student who has an active plan for the month you select. Period is year-month (example: 2026-04). Use a unique run key if you need to run again safely.
fees.v2LedgerDesc: Timeline of charges and payments for each student.
fees.bulkAssignTitle: Create fee records for many students at once (e.g. new term or admission batch)
fees.emptyStructures: No fee structures. Create one for each class band.
fees.bulkModalTitle: Add this fee for many students
fees.bulkWhatTitle: What happens when you click “Add fees for students”?
fees.bulkStep1: The system finds active students in the class (or section) you choose below.
fees.bulkStep2: For each of those students it adds one fee row with this plan’s amount and the due date you pick.
fees.bulkStep3: Those rows appear on the Payments tab and in the parent portal as amounts the family needs to pay.
fees.bulkStep4: No money is collected here—this step only records who owes what. You record payments later as usual.
fees.bulkStep5: If your school uses automated fee reminders, families may get a notice when a new fee is added (according to your settings).
fees.bulkSectionHelp: Pick one section if you are rolling this fee out to part of the class; otherwise leave “everyone” selected.
fees.bulkDueLabel: When should this fee be paid by?
fees.bulkDueHelp: This due date is stored on each student’s fee row. You can use it for reports, reminders, and overdue status.
fees.bulkSkipLabel: Skip students who already have this fee for the same due date (recommended). Turn off only if you intend to allow duplicate rows.
fees.bulkSkippedOne: {{n}} student was not given a new row (see reasons below).
fees.bulkSkippedMany: {{n}} students were not given a new row (see reasons below).
fees.bulkOpenPayments: Open the Payments tab to see the new rows.
fees.errComponents: Add at least one component with a name and amount.
fees.errTotalZero: Total fee amount must be greater than zero.
fees.errDuplicateComponent: Component names must be unique within a structure.
fees.txTimelineEmpty: No transaction entries yet for this obligation.
fees.refundAmountInvalid: Enter a valid refund amount greater than zero.
fees.refundAmountTooHigh: Refund amount cannot exceed collected amount.
fees.emptyLedger: No payment records found for this student.
fees.collectAmountInvalid: Enter a valid amount greater than zero.
fees.collectAmountTooHigh: Amount cannot be more than current due.
fees.skipDuplicate: already has this fee with the same due date
fees.bulkError: Could not add fees. Please try again or contact support.
fees.confirmDeleteMessage: Structure “{{name}}” will be removed from configuration. Existing fee payment records are not deleted.

chat.leadStaff: Secure messaging for teacher–parent updates, admin coordination, and support threads. Live delivery when the server is connected; same REST shape with mocks for local demos.
chat.leadSuperAdmin: Platform operator inbox: message campus administrators only. Search shows each admin with their school name and code. Same APIs as production; mocks use the platform school directory.
chat.statusWsTitle: WebSocket inbox and conversation topics push new messages without refresh.
chat.toolbarNoMatches: No people matched. Try another spelling or use New for more options.
chat.dirHelpSuperAdmin: Type at least two characters to find an active school admin. Results list name · school (code) so you can tell workspaces apart.
chat.dirHelpStaff: Search the same directory as Staff and people: teachers show homeroom context; students include parent chat when a parent login is linked.
chat.dirHelpParent: Parents can start chats only with their children's class teachers — use the child selector below (directory search is disabled for your role).
chat.plaSearchPh: Search by admin name, email, school name, or code…
chat.dirSearchPh: Type at least 2 characters (name, email, admission #)…
chat.noDirectoryData: No directory data available for your role yet.
chat.selectConversationHint: Choose a thread to load history and send messages. New replies push in real time when connected.
chat.emptyThreadHint: Open the conversation with a short note. Participants see the same thread; tenant and role checks apply on the server.
chat.composePlaceholder: Write a message… (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline)
chat.policyFootnote: End-to-end policy: only conversation participants can read or post; switching accounts or tenants never leaks threads across schools.
chat.emptyInboxHint: Start a thread from New or wait for an inbound message. With a live backend, updates also arrive over WebSocket.
chat.threadSubtitleDirect: Direct message — only you and this contact are in the thread.

leave.lead: Request time off with full or half-day options. Track balances and history. School admins review and approve the team queue; teachers only see their own requests here.
leave.policyCardHint: Set annual, sick, and casual days per policy cycle. Values apply to balance cards for all staff in this browser session (mock); the live API will use the same fields from tenant HR policy.
leave.balanceFootnote: Balances follow standard leave types (annual, sick, casual). "Other" requests are not counted in these buckets until HR maps them in policy.
leave.helpOtherReason: When you choose Other, describe the situation in full in the reason box (at least {{min}} characters). HR and approvers rely on this text.
leave.errOtherReasonRequired: Please enter a clear reason of at least {{min}} characters when leave type is Other.
leave.errSubmitGeneric: Could not submit the leave request. Try again.
leave.searchMinePh: Leave type, dates, or words from your reason…
leave.searchTeamPh: Applicant name, role, type, status, or dates…
leave.bulkAllocationHint: Run opening leave credits in bulk for teachers or staff. This is the yearly allocation used by enterprise HR systems.
leave.bulkOverwrite: Re-allocate even if this year already has opening entries
leave.ledgerHint: Every credit/debit is tracked here (opening allocation, approvals, reversals).
leave.ledgerEmpty: No ledger entries yet. Ask admin to run opening balance allocation for this year.

payroll.leadAdmin: Manage salary structures, payroll processing, payslips, and payout status from one screen.
payroll.leadTeacher: Filter payslips by period and download the official PDF after status is Paid. Salary documents are kept under Payroll, not in Profile.
payroll.leadDenied: Payroll is available to administrators and teachers.
payroll.adminFlowStep3: Step 3: Start payout (if enabled) or Mark paid after paying outside
payroll.errPeriodInvalid: Select a valid payroll period (month and year).
payroll.errFuturePeriodNotAllowed: Future payroll periods are not allowed.
payroll.errHistoryWindow: You can generate and disburse only for current month or previous month.

payroll.searchTeachersPh: Search by teacher, bank, or IFSC...
payroll.pdfTeacherRequiresPaid: Official payslip PDF is released only after salary is credited (Paid).

payroll.digitalPayoutOnStatus: Sending salary from Payroll is on for this school.
payroll.cardDisburseLead: Pick a teacher or use the table. Generate payslips for the month first, then use Start payout to request the bank transfer. Use Mark paid only after you have confirmed the salary reached their account.
payroll.digitalPayoutOffTitle: Sending salary from Payroll is off
payroll.digitalPayoutOffBody: Pay staff with your usual bank, cash, or payroll process, then open each payslip and tap Mark paid once the money has cleared. To show Start payout here, turn the option on under Settings → Finance & payments (after your payout account is set up on the server).
payroll.settlementModeHint: Whether this payslip was paid through Payroll’s bank send, or recorded after you paid outside the app (Mark paid).
payroll.confirmMarkPaidDetailOffline: The payslip will show “Outside app (mark paid)” as the settlement type. Use this when you already sent salary from your bank, cash, or any process that did not go through the in-app payout button.
payroll.generateFirst: Generate payslips for this period first.
payroll.salaryAlreadyDisbursed: Salary already disbursed for this period.
payroll.checkBankProfile: Bank profile has account holder, bank name, and account details.
payroll.checkIfsc: IFSC code format is valid (11 characters).
payroll.checkPayslip: Payslip is generated for selected month and year.
payroll.disburseFixBankHint: Update bank profile fields in teacher record.
payroll.disburseInvalidAccountHint: Open teacher bank profile and save an account number with 6 to 22 digits only.

payroll.previewStep2Body: Queue remains Submitted until webhook or manual settlement update confirms success/failure.
payroll.previewStep3Body: Mark payslip as paid only after salary credit is confirmed in bank statement.
payroll.previewAck: I reviewed these details and I want to start payout for this teacher.
payroll.afterBankNote: After bank credit confirmation, mark the payslip as paid below.
payroll.initiateTitle: Uses net amount from the generated payslip for selected month/year
payroll.structureModalLead: Choose the teacher, set monthly basic pay, then add optional allowance and deduction lines (same idea as fee components). Preview shows net pay. Each teacher can have one structure.
payroll.structureNoTeachersLeft: Every active teacher already has a salary structure, or no teachers were found. Add teachers first, or remove an existing structure before creating a new one.
payroll.structureLoadTeachersError: Could not load teachers. Try Refresh, then open again.
payroll.structureErrBasic: Enter a basic salary greater than zero.
payroll.payslipsLead: Filtered by the period above. Teachers can download the official payslip PDF only after status is Paid (salary settled). Admins: mark paid after you confirm the bank credit.
payroll.accessDenied: You do not have access to payroll details.
payroll.webhookSyncedTooltip: Status last synced from payout callback

reports.insightsStep3Lead: Review your selections and run insights.

reminders.lead: Outstanding balances are listed below. In production, an automated schedule sends SMS/WhatsApp/email as due dates approach. This screen reflects that reminder workflow and supports manual triggering.
reminders.emptyPending: No pending fee balances in the current fee ledger.
reminders.emptyQueue: No reminders are scheduled yet. Open this tab again to run the mock auto-scheduler for dues within 14 days.
reminders.searchQueuePh: Student name, id, channel, or status…
reminders.errDuplicatePendingReminder: A pending reminder already exists for this student and channel.

importExport.pillTitle: Only school administrators and platform super-admins can use this module
importExport.lead: Queue CSV, XLSX, or ZIP (CSV inside) jobs in the background—batched and non-blocking. Download exports in the same shape for round-trip edits. Run a dry-run first, then queue; see per-row outcomes and retry only what failed.
importExport.newJobLead: Pick a dataset, drop a ZIP (contains the CSV below), then queue.
importExport.newJobLeadV2: Pick a dataset, upload CSV/XLSX or a ZIP that contains the template CSV, then validate or queue. Suggested order: 1 Classes → 2 Teachers → 3 Staff (library) → 4 Students → 5 Timetable → 6 Fee structures. For teachers/staff CSV you may add optional column schoolrolecodes (comma-separated short codes from Settings → Staff duties & access, e.g. FEE_OFFICE,ACADEMIC_STAFF) when a portal user is created on the row.
importExport.targetSchoolCodeHint: Enter the exact school code so import runs in the correct school workspace.
importExport.targetSchoolCodeRequired: School code is required for Super Admin import operations.
importExport.executionModeBestEffort: Best effort (default — each row in its own transaction)
importExport.executionModeAllOrNothing: All or nothing (single database transaction for the whole file)
importExport.executionModeHint: All-or-nothing undoes the entire import if any row fails—best for smaller, safety-critical files. Best effort scales better for very large files.
importExport.schoolSelectBeforeUpload: Select a school code first to enable header preview, dry run, and import queue.
importExport.mapLead: Match each file column to a system field. Required fields for this job type must be mapped before validate or queue.
importExport.mapIncomplete: Map all required fields for this job type (see template export for column names).
importExport.previewFailed: Could not read file headers. Check the file format.
importExport.metricsLead: Last 24 hours for this school (from the database). JVM-wide counters and histograms are on /actuator/metrics (Prometheus: /actuator/prometheus).
importExport.metricsPromHint: Platform metrics namespace: {{ns}} — use for Grafana dashboards.
importExport.dropSubBefore: or click to browse · must include
importExport.dropSubV2: or click to browse · filename must match
importExport.reprocessToggleLabel: Treat this upload as corrective patch reprocess
importExport.reprocessToggleHint: Use only when you need to rerun the same file/mapping for UPSERT corrections. Backend must allow reprocess override in this environment.
importExport.dryRunTeacherMissing: Teacher reference was not found in your teacher directory for this school. Import teachers first, or use the correct teacher id/phone/email.
importExport.dryRunTeacherDoubleBooked: The same teacher is assigned to more than one class in the same day and period. Move one class to another period or assign another teacher.
importExport.timetableDryRunHelper: Sales tip: first import teachers successfully, then run timetable dry-run. If many sections run in the same period, make sure you have enough unique teachers for that period.
importExport.msgDryRunDone: Dry run completed. Review the summary before queuing.
importExport.exportsLead: Download current roster data—columns match bulk import templates.
importExport.exportStudentsDesc: Names, placement (class/section IDs or names + year), admission, guardians, importmode—matches bulk template.
importExport.exportTeachersDesc: Profile, portal flags, subjects, bank fields, importmode—matches bulk template.
importExport.exportStaffDesc: Non-teaching staff in canonical re-import structure.
importExport.exportFeeDesc: Latest fee structures with component_spec for UPSERT re-import.
importExport.msgExportTimeout: Export is taking longer than expected. Please refresh status shortly.
importExport.jobsReportLead: Job summary and per-row outcomes—open Lines to inspect payloads and retry failed rows only.
importExport.emptyJobsLead: Upload a CSV, XLSX, or ZIP above to run your first async import.
importExport.msgNormalizedDownloadFailed: Could not download normalized CSV for this job.
importExport.undoLedgerEmpty: No ledger entries for this job yet (or still processing).

onboard.success: Workspace created. Tenant: {{tenantId}} · Academic year ID: {{academicYearId}}
onboard.flowSuccessTitle: School and admin workspace created successfully

hints.academicYearLabel: Use a clear year label your school team will recognize.
hints.academicYearSelect: Select the academic year; start and end dates are auto-filled and can still be adjusted.
hints.academicYearStart: Pick the first day of the academic year.
hints.academicYearEnd: Pick the final day; this must be after start date.
hints.address: Optional but recommended for support and sales handover.

errors.academicYearStartRequired: Academic year start date is required.
errors.academicYearEndRequired: Academic year end date is required.
errors.academicYearRange: End date must be after start date.
errors.fixFieldErrors: Please fix highlighted fields before submitting.
errors.createFailed: Unable to onboard school workspace right now. Please retry.

jobTypeHint.STAFF: staff.csv — after teachers, before students; library roles
jobTypeHint.TIMETABLE: Weekly slots by teacher, class, and section (6-7 periods/day)
jobTypeHint.FEE_STRUCTURES: Upsert by class + year + structure name (componentspec: Name:Amount:Type | ...)

schoolOnboarding.lead: Follow these steps in order so academic structure exists before you bulk-import students and staff.
schoolOnboarding.footerHint: Use Academic to create the current year, classes, and sections first. Then run School onboarding with a dry-run before large jobs.
schoolOnboarding.step1Body: Create or mark the active academic year—imports resolve placement against it when year is omitted.
schoolOnboarding.step2Body: Define classes and sections so roster rows can match by ID or by class and section name.
schoolOnboarding.step3Body: Import students, teachers, or class lists via async jobs; validate with dry-run for large files.
schoolOnboarding.step4Body: Confirm branding, locale, and feature flags so the portal matches your school.

transport.lead: Routes, fleet, drivers, ordered stops, assignments, and live map
transport.noGps: No live GPS — showing planned area
transport.assignedScrollRegion: Students on this route — scroll to see all
transport.emptyRoutes: No routes yet. Add a route as admin (works with mocks or API).
transport.mapTip: Tip: use “Simulate GPS” to move the pin to a live position.
transport.stopsHelp: Define pickup/drop order now; you can edit stops later from the route card.
transport.errPhoneInvalidTenDigits: Phone number must be exactly 10 digits.
transport.assignStudentHelp: Choose a learner, then pick where they board and leave the vehicle. Students already on this route are hidden.
transport.assignNoStopsWarning: Add at least one stop to this route before assigning students.
transport.assignNoEligibleStudents: No students match your search, or everyone listed is already assigned to this route.
transport.confirmDeleteMessage: Route “{{name}}” will be removed. Student assignments and stops linked to this route may need to be reviewed.

library.lead: Catalog, circulation, and copy counts stay in sync when you issue or return
library.readOnlyHint: Library catalog changes and circulation are limited to administrators and designated library staff (assistant, librarian, or head).
library.readOnlyHintTeacher: You can browse the catalog and circulation. Adding or removing books, issuing copies, and processing returns are limited to school administrators and teachers with a designated library duty.
library.readOnlyHintMember: You are in member mode: browse catalog and view your own borrow status/history only. Circulation and catalog changes are desk-only.
library.readOnlyHintSuperAdmin: Platform administrators manage schools only. In-tenant library operations are performed by each school’s admin and library staff.
library.fineHelp: Leave per-day rate empty to use the school default from settings / tenant config.
library.noStudentsLoaded: No students loaded. Use Refresh, or check your account permissions.
library.confirmInactiveMessage: “{{title}}” will disappear from the active catalog and cannot be issued until it is restored.

hostel.lead: Rooms by capacity (1–4 beds), live occupancy, allocations
hostel.buildingsLead: Real-time free beds per building. Add rooms against the correct hostel so capacity stays accurate.
hostel.vacateHint: This frees one bed for new allocations, same as the allocate flow.
hostel.capacityBelowOccupancy: Capacity cannot be set below current occupancy ({{n}}).

settings.leadUser: Your profile and read-only school information. Tenant changes are limited to school administrators.
settings.financeHubLead: Choose how this school collects fees and where online payments settle. Your portrait and sign-in details stay under Profile.
settings.financeOverviewLead: This workspace is designed around high-volume UPI/card fee collection with clear settlement ownership. Use the Fee settlement tab for live Razorpay Route configuration.
settings.financeOverviewBullet1: Parent checkout and webhooks stay tenant-scoped; settlement mode decides where captured funds land.
settings.financeOverviewBullet2: Payroll payouts and parent fee checkout can use different rails — keep operational notes in Internal notes for audit handover.
settings.financeOverviewBullet3: More gateways (e.g. additional aggregators) and payout automation can plug in here as separate sub-sections without moving controls back into Profile.
settings.financeLead: When using Razorpay Route, parent payments can settle to the school's linked account instead of the platform merchant. Configure the linked account id from your Razorpay dashboard after onboarding the school.
settings.financeParentCheckoutLead: Turn this off while parents pay at the school counter; staff records payments in Fees. Turn on when the school is ready for Razorpay (or other) checkout from the parent portal. This is independent of settlement mode above.
settings.financeParentCheckoutCheckbox: Allow parents to pay fees online from the parent portal (when the platform has checkout enabled)
settings.financeSettlementLead: One place to choose counter collection versus online checkout, and—when you use Razorpay Route—where funds settle.
settings.financePayrollPayoutLead: Not related to parent fee payments. This only controls whether Payroll can trigger a bank transfer to staff, or should only record salaries you already paid elsewhere.
settings.financePayrollPayoutToggle: Allow sending staff salary from Payroll (digital payout)
settings.financePayrollPayoutHelpWhenOff: With this off: Payroll hides Start payout. Pay teachers yourself, then use Mark paid so payslips stay accurate.
settings.financePayrollPayoutHelpWhenOn: With this on: after payslips exist, Start payout sends the transfer using your deployment’s payout setup. You can still use Mark paid if someone was paid outside Payroll.
settings.financePayrollPayoutOffHint: Typical when salaries always leave the school’s own bank or cash desk. Payslips, PDFs, and reports work the same.
settings.financePayrollPayoutOnHint: Payroll will show Start payout and the settlement queue. Your hosting team must have connected a payout provider (for example RazorpayX) for transfers to actually work.
settings.financePayrollPayoutEnableConfirmMsg: You are about to allow sending salary from the Payroll page. A payout account must already be configured for this environment; admins will see Start payout when this is on.
settings.financePayrollPayoutEnableDetail1: Payroll will show the payout queue and Start payout for payslips in the current period.
settings.financePayrollPayoutEnableDetail2: You can still use Mark paid when you paid someone outside the app; each payslip records how it was settled.
settings.financePayrollPayoutEnableDetail3: Turn this off anytime if the school only wants to record salary in the portal after paying from its own bank.
settings.financePayrollPayoutDisableConfirmMsg: You are about to turn off sending salary from Payroll. Start payout will be hidden; pay staff your usual way, then use Mark paid in Payroll.
settings.financePayrollPayoutDisableDetail1: In-progress payout attempts in the queue remain visible for reconciliation until resolved.
settings.financePayrollPayoutDisableDetail2: Payslips, PDFs, and notifications for “mark paid” are unchanged.
settings.financePayrollPayoutDisableDetail3: You can re-enable in-app transfer later when the school is ready to connect a payout service.
settings.financeSettlementModeHelp: Changing mode may turn parent-portal online checkout on or off. Save to apply; we will confirm before updating.
settings.financeModeOfflineExplain: Parents do not pay through the parent portal. Staff record cash, UPI, or card at the counter in Fees; families still see balances and receipts in the portal.
settings.financeModePlatformExplain: When the platform enables checkout, parents can pay online from the parent portal. Captures settle on the platform merchant unless you switch to Route.

financeSaveConfirmBody.OFFLINE_SCHOOL_COLLECTION: You are switching to pay-at-school. Parent-portal online checkout will turn off; keep recording collections in Fees.
financeSaveConfirmBody.PLATFORM_MERCHANT: You are switching to platform-merchant settlement. Route-only fields are cleared until you pick Route again; online checkout can follow platform rules.
financeSaveConfirmBody.ROUTE_LINKED_ACCOUNT: You are switching to Razorpay Route. Linked account and commission apply; onboarding status below controls when parents can pay on that rail.

settings.financeCommissionHelp: 0–10000 (100 = 1%). The remainder of each capture stays on the platform merchant; the routed share goes to the linked account.
settings.financeNotesSaved: Internal notes saved. Settlement fields were not changed.
settings.financeNoChangesNotes: Update internal notes, or use “Change Route settlement” to edit linked account, mode, or commission.
settings.financeLiveLockedBody: To protect parent payments, linked account, settlement mode, and commission are locked. You can still update internal notes, or unlock to edit—saving settlement changes will pause Route checkout until you submit again and the platform re-approves.
settings.financeLiveEditModeBody: Changes to mode, linked account, or commission will move you to “pending re-review” and pause parent Route online pay until approved again. Cancel to discard unsaved edits.
settings.financeLiveEditConfirmTitle: Pause parent Route payments to apply changes?
settings.financeLiveEditConfirmBody: Saving these settlement changes will require submitting for platform review again. Parent online fee payments on the Route path will be paused until the platform marks you LIVE.
settings.financeValidationRouteRequiresAcc: Route settlement needs a Razorpay linked account id (starts with acc_).
settings.financeValidationAccFormat: Linked account id must look like acc_ followed only by letters and numbers (at least 12 characters total).
settings.financeValidationBpsRange: Platform commission must be a whole number between 0 and 10000 (basis points).
settings.financeOnbStep1: Select Route settlement and enter the Razorpay linked account id (acc_…) from your Razorpay dashboard after the school is onboarded under the platform.
settings.financeOnbStep2: Save your settings. If you later change the linked account or commission while LIVE, the system will ask you to submit again before parents can pay.
settings.financeOnbStep3: Complete the declaration and submit for platform review. Parent card/UPI checkout on the Route rail stays paused until the platform marks this workspace LIVE.
settings.financeOnbSubmittedLead: Under platform review. Parents cannot pay via the Route-linked school rail online until approval.
settings.financeOnbLiveLead: LIVE — Route-linked parent checkout is enabled for this workspace.
settings.financeOnbPendingLead: Settlement details changed after LIVE. Submit again for platform review before parents can pay online.
settings.financeOnbDeclarationPh: Example: We, [legal name], confirm this linked account is ours / our trust’s, KYC is complete, and we authorize fee settlements to this account under the platform agreement.
settings.financeOnbDeclarationHint: Minimum 30 characters, maximum 2000. Stored when you submit for review.
settings.financeOnbSubmitOk: Submitted for platform review. Parents remain paused on Route until the platform sets LIVE.
settings.financeOnbWithdrawOk: Submission withdrawn. You may edit settlement settings again.
settings.profileLead: Your portrait and account details — the same identity shown in the header and menus.
settings.accountDetailsViewLead: Review your sign-in identity and verification status. Select Edit to update your login email, phone, and profile details.
settings.accountDetailsLead: Email and phone changes are identity-sensitive. New email requires verification; new phone is verified on OTP login.
settings.profileEmailReadonlyHint: Sign-in email — change only through your IT or platform administrator.
settings.profileEditableEmailHint: Changing email triggers verification before email/password login is allowed.
settings.profileIdentityFlowHint: You can login via phone OTP anytime. Email/password login requires verified email and a password.
settings.emailVerifyLead: After verification you can use email sign-in and password reset on email. In development, the server may show a one-time token to paste below.
settings.emailVerifyDone: Email verified. Your profile is updated.
settings.emailVerifyErr: Could not complete verification. Try again or contact support.
settings.phoneVerifyViaOtpHint: Phone verification completes automatically on your next OTP login.
settings.profileSavedMock: Account details saved in this session (mock). Same request will call PUT /auth/profile when mocks are off.
settings.profileSaveErr: Could not save account details. Try again.
settings.profileConfirmBody: Please confirm these sensitive account changes before saving. These updates affect your profile identity and may impact sign-in verification.
settings.identityImpactConfirmTitle: This update changes your login identity
settings.identityImpactConfirmBody: Review the sign-in impact before saving. These changes will apply to your next login flow.
settings.identityImpactEmailLine: Email changed: email/password login stays blocked until the new email is verified.
settings.identityImpactPhoneLine: Phone changed: next OTP login must use the new phone number and will re-verify phone.
settings.securityActionsLead: Set or rotate your password after phone/email verification. Keep this password private.
settings.setPasswordMinLength: Use at least 8 characters for your password.
settings.setPasswordMismatch: Password and confirmation do not match.
settings.setPasswordSuccess: Password set successfully. You can now use email/password login (after email verification).
settings.setPasswordErr: Password could not be set. Verify phone/email first and retry.
settings.setPasswordConfirmMessage: You are about to set the password for this account. This is a sensitive security change.
settings.setPasswordConfirmDetail1: Your next sign-in must use this new password wherever password login is offered.
settings.setPasswordConfirmDetail2: If you also use phone OTP, you can keep using it; this password is an additional way to sign in.
settings.setPasswordConfirmDetail3: If your email is not verified yet, finish email verification before relying on email + password login.
settings.credentialsUpdatedDialogBody: Use the updated contact details from the next login onward.
settings.profilePhoneInvalidTenDigits: Contact phone must be exactly 10 digits.
settings.branchesLoadErr: Could not load branches. Try again or check your connection.
settings.generalSavedMock: Saved on this device (mock). Same fields will sync from the API when mocks are off.
settings.uploadPhotoAria: Upload or change your profile photo
settings.photoHintManaged: Your portrait is managed by school staff — not edited on this screen.
settings.schoolCodeTitle: Used to link branches in multi-campus setups
settings.branchesLead: Schools that share your school code appear here (read-only directory). Each branch logs in as its own tenant.
settings.photoHintMock: Shown in the header and menus. Saved on this browser only until your school connects cloud photos.
settings.photoHintLive: Shown in the header and menus. Stored with your account when you save; full media sync may follow.
settings.directoryPhotoBullet1: Admins and class teachers update official photos where your role allows.
settings.directoryPhotoBullet2: Contact the office if you need a change.
settings.linkedChildrenHintLong: Pick a child to see their class, section, and class teacher from the school roster. Optional photo below is only for your parent portal view — not the official record.
settings.selectChildForDetails: Select a child to view their school details.
settings.optionalPortalPhotoHint: Only shown to you in the app until the school enables cloud photos.
settings.directoryPhotosUpdatedByStaff: Official directory photos are updated by staff under Students or Teachers.
settings.contactOfficeForUpdates: Contact your school office if any detail needs updating.
settings.noOtherBranches: No other branches returned for this code.
settings.brandingPersistNote: Persists primary/accent to this browser and updates CSS variables. With API, also saves to tenant settings (school admin only).
settings.rolesHeading: Who can use which parts of the school app

rbac.lead: Use this page to match each staff member with the day-to-day tasks they are trusted to perform in the app—such as fees, exams, library, transport, or payroll. You are not changing how they log in; you are only turning specific work areas on or off for their account.
rbac.leadSub: Tip: pick the person first, tap “Choose their duties”, tick the roles that fit their job, then save. If you use Excel onboarding, the same role names can be listed in the staff CSV column schoolrolecodes.
rbac.mockBanner: You are in preview mode on this device. Changes here do not reach the live school until your administrator connects the app to the real server.
rbac.selectStaff: Select someone from the staff list
rbac.packsHint: A “bundle” groups several tasks together so you can reuse the same mix on more than one custom role. Most schools can rely on the built-in roles below; add bundles only if your office needs a repeatable combination.
rbac.fieldPackCode: Short code (CAPITAL letters, no spaces—used in imports and reports)
rbac.packPermsLabel: Tick every task this bundle should include.
rbac.composeLabel: How do you want to build this role?
rbac.composeDirect: Tick tasks one by one (good for small changes)
rbac.composeGroups: Attach one or more bundles (good when the same mix is reused often)
rbac.groupsPickerHint: You may select more than one bundle. The person receives every task that appears in any selected bundle.
rbac.deletePackConfirm: Delete this bundle? Any custom role that still points to it will stop receiving those tasks until you edit the role.
rbac.catalogHint: Built-in roles describe common school desks (fee office, exam cell, and so on). Add a custom role only when none of the ready-made options fit—your sales or IT partner can help name them clearly.
rbac.assignSummaryHint: Press the button above, tick the roles, then press Save in the window. Nothing changes until you save.
rbac.assignSummaryNone: No extra duties yet—only their basic staff or teacher access.
rbac.assignModalSubtitle: Tick every role that matches what this person does in real life. They will only see menus and buttons for those areas. Ask them to sign out and sign back in (or wait for an automatic refresh) before testing.
rbac.fieldCode: Short code (CAPITAL letters, unique—used in CSV import and reports)
rbac.permsLabel: Tick each task this role may perform. When unsure, start small—you can add more later.
rbac.deleteConfirm: Delete this custom role? Anyone who only had this role will lose those menus until you assign a different role.
rbac.recentAuditEmpty: No duty changes were recorded in the last month.

settings.rbacUserShellNote: Only a school administrator can change someone else’s duties. If you do not see this tab, open Profile to review your own account.
settings.rbacDeskNoAssignmentPrivilege: Your role covers school settings and finance profile, but not assigning staff duties. Ask a school administrator to change role assignments.
settings.roleParentScope: Child progress, fees, and communication follow-up
settings.featureTogglesLeadHtml: Turn modules on or off for your school. Use Save feature toggles so changes apply on the server (or local mock store).
settings.platformModulesLead: These switches are controlled by your platform operator (super admin). They define which optional modules appear in the sidebar for your school.
settings.frameAriaOptionalChildPhoto: Optional child photo for parent portal
settings.featureFlagsSavedMock: Saved locally (mock). Same keys sync to PUT /settings/features when mocks are off.

transport.name: Transport module
transport.description: Manage school transport routes and vehicles.

directory.name: Directory
directory.description: Unified admin search across students and staff.

parentPortal.childrenPageSubtitle: Select a child and month for attendance; fees and receipts stay per child. Overview and guidance are on your Dashboard.
parentPortal.detailLead: On this page, you can check your selected child's monthly attendance, fee due details, and payment receipts. For a quick overall summary and recent updates, open your Dashboard.
parentPortal.leadBefore: Track attendance, fees, and performance. Exams, timetables, and published grades live under
parentPortal.leadAfter: in the sidebar. Fee payments are per selected child.
parentPortal.attendanceMonthHint: Summary and the table use this calendar month (through today if it is the current month).
parentPortal.resultsTileAria: Open published examination results for the selected child
parentPortal.examsBannerMid: : pick your child, then each exam’s
parentPortal.paymentActivityLead: Official receipts for the selected period (download HTML for your records).
parentPortal.emptyReceiptsLead: Adjust dates or complete a payment — ledger receipts appear here.
parentPortal.payableSubtext: Includes overdue balance and late fee.
parentPortal.payHintMocksMid: Razorpay is a preview tile — use a real parent login (useMocks: false) to run checkout.
parentPortal.payHintMocksHtml: Instant (demo) records a payment in-browser. Razorpay is a preview tile — use a real parent login (useMocks: false) to run checkout.
parentPortal.payHintProd: Card, UPI, and netbanking run in Razorpay’s secure window. The school is notified only after your bank authorizes the payment.
parentPortal.razorpayMissingKeys: Razorpay is not configured on the server (set RAZORPAY_KEY / RAZORPAY_SECRET on the API).
parentPortal.razorpayWindowHint: A secure Razorpay window opens for card, UPI, or netbanking (including bank OTP). If it did not appear, allow pop-ups or use the button below.
parentPortal.devMockHint: Dev: completing here updates in-browser mock fee state only (mock login is not a JWT).
parentPortal.payAtSchoolTitle: Online payment is not enabled for this school — pay fees at the school office
parentPortal.payAtSchoolHint: After you pay at the school, the office will record it here. Your balance and receipts will update in this portal.
parentPortal.feesV2PaymentSubmitted: Payment authorized. The school will confirm it in a few moments—balances refresh after the payment is processed.

razorpay.label: Razorpay
razorpay.hint: UPI, cards & netbanking in a secure window (production)
razorpay.hintMock: Preview tile — use real login (useMocks: false) to pay

mockpay.label: Instant (demo)
mockpay.hint: Completes in-browser — no school API (mock portal only)

err.amountPositive: Amount must be greater than zero.
err.nothingPayable: Nothing is payable for this fee right now.
err.razorpayPreviewMock: Razorpay is preview-only in mock portal. Choose Instant (demo) to record a payment, or set useMocks to false for real checkout.
err.orderNoKey: Order created but Razorpay publishable key is missing on the API. Set app.payments.razorpay.key or RAZORPAY_KEY, then try again.
err.checkoutFailed: Could not create checkout. Use valid Razorpay keys on the API, amount ≤ payable, and a real parent login.
err.methodUnavailable: This payment method is not available.
err.rzpInrOnly: Razorpay checkout supports INR only in this integration.
err.confirmFailed: Confirmation failed. If your account was debited, contact the school with your bank reference.
err.receiptPdfFailed: Could not download the fee receipt PDF. Try again or contact the school.

forgotPassword.title: Reset your password
forgotPassword.subtitle: Enter your school code and registered mobile number. We will send an OTP so you can set a new password safely.
forgotPassword.otpSent: OTP sent. Please check your mobile number.
forgotPassword.otpVerified: OTP verified. Set your new password.
forgotPassword.doneLead: Your password has been updated. You can sign in with your new password.
forgotPassword.heroSubtitle: A simple OTP-first reset flow keeps school accounts secure without making families or staff wait.
forgotPassword.errorSend: Could not send reset OTP. Check school code and mobile number, then try again.
forgotPassword.errorVerify: Could not verify OTP. Try again or request a new code.
forgotPassword.errorReset: Could not reset the password. Please request a fresh OTP and try again.

verifyEmail.title: Verify your email
verifyEmail.subtitle: We are confirming the link from your inbox. This only takes a moment.
verifyEmail.success: Your email is verified. You can continue to the app or sign in again.
verifyEmail.errGeneric: We could not verify this link. It may have expired or already been used. Request a new verification email from account settings.
verifyEmail.errMissingToken: This page needs a verification link from your email. Open the link we sent you, or sign in and request a new verification email.
verifyEmail.heroSubtitle: Email verification helps protect school accounts and keeps important notices reaching the right inbox.
